figures.caption: Figure 8: Agreement of PartField with our STEP-based segmentation as a function of model complexity (measured by the number of STEP parts). Bars show mean overall accuracy, mean IoU, and boundary accuracy of PartField with respect to the STEP reference, grouped by complexity bin. (Sec. 4.4)
Figure 11: Comparison of a subset of models on their STEP-partitioning versus PartField segmentation outcomes under different tessellation levels.
Figure 4: Qualitative comparison of segmentation boundaries on ABC models. Left: PartField (meshbased segmentation). Right: STEP-Parts (B-Repdriven).

Figure 7: Metric tradeoffs under dihedral-threshold perturbations. Each point is one shape; colors indicate the threshold θ ∈ {4◦, 6◦, 8◦, 10◦, 12◦}. Left: intra-part deviation Dintra versus boundary sharpness Sboundary. Right: part-count P versus part-size entropy H. The near-complete overlap of colors indicates that these aggregate metrics are effectively invariant to θ in the tested low-angle regime.

Figure 5: Global distribution of dihedral angles ϕ over all same-primitive face adjacencies in our ABC/DeepCAD subset. The mass concentrates near ϕ ≈ 90◦ (sharp features), with a sparse near-zero tail (tangent-continuous joins / small numerical effects). Some models contain no sameprimitive adjacencies.

Figure 9: Distribution of per-shape mean IoU between PartField and the STEP-based segmentation after optimal label permutation (Sec. 4). The dashed line marks the global mean (≈ 0.10), indicating that most shapes exhibit limited partwise alignment.

Figure 10: Per-shape comparison of the number of parts observed in samples for the STEP-based segmentation and the PartField segmentation. Each point corresponds to one STEP/PartField pair; the dashed line indicates perfect agreement.

Figure 6: Local stability of STEP-Parts under threshold perturbations. Partition statistics and auxiliary quality metrics remain effectively unchanged for θ ∈ {4◦, 6◦, 8◦, 10◦, 12◦}, indicating a stable low-angle operating regime rather than sensitivity to a finely tuned threshold.
Figure 12: Comparison of a subset of models on their STEP-partitioning versus PartField segmentation. From left to right: ground truth, PartField, our method
